title: ROI Scoring and Backlog Prioritization -- automated dispatch ordering
what: The ROI scorer computes a priority score for each open lug by calculating roi = impact / effort. Lugs are sorted by urgency tier first (1=URGENT dispatched before tier 2), then by roi descending within each tier. The sorted queue is what Ozi reads for dispatch ordering.
why: Without ROI scoring, Ozi would dispatch in arbitrary or filesystem order. Scoring ensures the highest-value work executes first, and urgent items preempt lower-urgency items regardless of roi.

use_cases.title: Prioritizing a queue of open lugs
use_cases.outcome: Lugs sorted: all urgency=1 first, then urgency=2 by roi, etc. Ozi dispatches top N within session budget

use_cases.title: Vibe affinity tiebreaking
use_cases.trigger: Two lugs with identical urgency and roi
use_cases.outcome: va (vibe_affinity) field used as tiebreaker: build/fix/think/grind/ship

patterns.name: Urgency tier before ROI
patterns.description: urgency=1 (URGENT) dispatches before any urgency=2 item, even if urgency=2 has higher ROI. Tiers are absolute priority buckets.

patterns.name: Effort calibration matters
patterns.description: effort=1 vs effort=5 on an impact=8 lug changes roi from 8.0 to 1.6. Miscalibrated effort scores distort the entire queue.

how.trigger: Called by Ozi at start of nightly run, or manually via score_backlog.py / lathe_score.py
steps_summary: 1. Scan bytype/*/open/ for all open lugs
2. For each lug: compute roi = impact / effort (default impact=5, effort=3 if missing)
3. Sort: primary = urgency ascending (1 first), secondary = roi descending
4. Apply va tiebreak for identical urgency+roi
5. Output ordered list to Ozi dispatch queue
constraints: impact defaults to 5, effort defaults to 3 if absent - always set them explicitly
ROI score is advisory - human can override dispatch order

open_questions: Which script is canonical: score_backlog.py or lathe_score.py?

body: The ROI scorer is the prioritization engine that converts an unordered set of open lugs into a dispatch-ordered queue. The scoring formula is: roi = impact / effort. Impact defaults to 5 and effort defaults to 3 when absent from the lug — these defaults are intentionally moderate to avoid artificially inflating or deflating unscored items. Urgency tier is the primary sort key: urgency=1 (URGENT) items dispatch before any urgency=2 items, urgency=2 before urgency=3, and so on — ROI is irrelevant across tier boundaries. Within a tier, items sort by roi descending. When two items share identical urgency and ROI, the va (vibe_affinity) field breaks the tie: build > ship > fix > grind > think, matching the most common user workflow preference. The scorer is invoked at the start of every Ozi nightly run via score_backlog.py or lathe_score.py; it is also available for manual backlog review. Inputs are all JSON files in bytype/*/open/. Outputs are an ordered list delivered to Ozi for dispatch sequencing. Items with effort=0 cause division-by-zero and are treated as effort=1 with an error annotation. Items with manual_gate=true in execute_when are excluded from the scored queue entirely — they appear only in the 'gated' section of the display. Items with unresolved blocked_by references are scored but marked as 'blocked' and skipped at dispatch time even if they would otherwise rank first. The scored output also marks items whose quality_score (from Ozi quality check) falls at or below 3 as 'needs_refinement' — they are displayed separately from the ready queue at wakeup.
done_when: Given two open lugs with urgency=1 and urgency=2, the urgency=1 lug appears first in the scored queue regardless of which has higher ROI
A lug with missing impact and effort fields scores at roi=5/3=1.67 (defaults applied) and appears in the queue without error
A lug with execute_when.manual_gate=true is excluded from the ready queue and appears only in the gated section
Two lugs with identical urgency and ROI are ordered by va field: a lug with va=build ranks above a lug with va=think
The scorer output correctly separates ready items (quality_score >3, no blockers) from needs_refinement items (quality_score <=3)
